text.title: Spring Boot Layered Failure Modes
text.summary: Four Spring Boot antipatterns. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: `@Transactional` on a controller method. DB transaction lifetime spans the full HTTP request including JSON serialisation; lazy collections trigger queries; long transactions hold row locks.
antipattern.root-cause: Author placed transaction boundary on the wrong layer.
antipattern.fix: Move `@Transactional` to the service method. Controller calls the service and returns the DTO; no transaction in the HTTP layer.

antipattern.symptom: Service method `sendEmail()` annotated `@Async` is called from another method in the SAME bean via `this.sendEmail()`. Runs synchronously; observed thread is the request thread.
antipattern.root-cause: Self-invocation bypasses the Spring AOP proxy; `@Async` is never applied.
antipattern.fix: Extract `@Async` methods into a separate bean. Inject that bean and call `emailSender.sendEmail(...)`. Add a named executor (`@Bean("emailExecutor")`) and `@Async("emailExecutor")`.

antipattern.symptom: Service signature `Order getOrder(UUID id)` returns the JPA entity. Controller serialises lazy associations; `LazyInitializationException` or N+1 in production.
antipattern.root-cause: Service skipped DTO mapping; entity leaked across the boundary.
antipattern.fix: Introduce `OrderResponse` record. Map inside the service (manually or via MapStruct). Controller returns the DTO.

antipattern.symptom: Controllers return `ApiErrorResponse{code, message, timestamp, â€¦}` with custom shape. Adding new endpoints duplicates the envelope; clients implement N parsers.
antipattern.root-cause: Author rolled own error envelope instead of using `ProblemDetail`.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `@RestControllerAdvice` mapping exceptions to `ProblemDetail` (RFC 7807). Single error format for all endpoints; OpenAPI generation handles it.
